people.text: Alex wants beginner-friendly music lessons with patient teachers and practical activities.
Jamie enjoys social evening activities connected with music and meeting new people.
Taylor prefers calm, organised environments with clear instructions and small groups.
Morgan needs an inexpensive option near public transport because they finish work late.
Casey enjoys creative activities and wants personal advice from experienced instructors.

texts.text: Experienced leaders provide [10]individual feedback[/10] and encourage participants to develop [10]creative personal ideas[/10].
This intensive programme prepares experienced users for national competitions.
The centre offers [9]low-cost sessions[/9] close to the main train station, making it useful for [9]people travelling after work[/9].
Members meet [7]on weekday evenings[/7] and spend time socialising before activities begin. The atmosphere is relaxed and friendly.
Weekend family sessions are mainly designed for children under twelve.
Advanced participants prepare independently and complete extra practice at home every week.
This course introduces [6]basic music skills[/6] through practical sessions. Teachers give [6]clear support for complete beginners[/6] and answer questions patiently.
This programme uses [8]small groups and carefully organised sessions[/8]. Staff provide [8]simple step-by-step instructions[/8] for nervous newcomers.

answers.explanation: Alex needs beginner lessons with practical support.
Casey wants creativity and personal feedback.
